concepts.title: Ocean's Water Salinity
concepts.description: The salinity of oceans varies from 3.2% to 3.8% due to factors like temperature, depth, and freshwater input.

concepts.title: Oceanic Zones
concepts.description: The ocean is divided into five zones - Intertidal, Subtidal, Bathyal, Abyssal, and Hadal zones.

concepts.title: Ocean Currents
concepts.description: Warm and cold ocean currents play a crucial role in regulating climate. Examples include the Gulf Stream and the Kuroshio Current.

concepts.title: Ocean Types
concepts.description: To remember the types of oceans - Pacific, Atlantic, Indian, Arctic, and Southern - use the mnemonic 'PAINS'.

concepts.title: Identifying Oceans
concepts.description: Students often confuse the Indian Ocean with the Pacific Ocean. Ensure to remember that the Indian Ocean is the third-largest ocean and is located between Africa and Asia.

concepts.title: Ocean Depth Measurements
concepts.description: The units used to measure ocean depth include fathoms, meters, and leagues. Remember that 1 fathom is equal to 6 feet or 1.8288 meters.

concepts.title: Ocean's Role in Weather
concepts.description: Oceans play a significant role in weather patterns and climate regulation. They cover about 71% of the Earth's surface and absorb a large amount of solar energy.

concepts.title: Ocean's Water Circulation
concepts.description: The ocean's water circulation is driven by wind, thermohaline circulation, and ocean currents. This circulation helps in the distribution of heat and nutrients around the globe.
